title: Pellaea brachyptera
taxonomy: Pellaea brachyptera belongs to the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the fern family. It is a species of fern in the genus Pellaea. Pellaea brachyptera is native to Australia and is often found growing in woodland and grassland environments. This plant is known to have fronds that are typically 20-50 cm in length and are dark green in color. The classification of Pellaea brachyptera is further broken down into the phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and subclass Polypodiidae.

intro: Pellaea brachyptera, also known as Australian brake fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pellaea brachyptera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Pellaea brachyptera include tea infusions, tinctures, and infusions used in traditional Chinese medicine and Australian bush medicine.
